differential_diagnoses: Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) with lupus nephritis (Class V) and secondary nephrotic syndrome
Antiphospholipid syndrome (APS) with bilateral renal vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism
Membranous nephropathy (primary) with nephrotic syndrome and hypercoagulable state
ANCA-associated vasculitis (e.g., microscopic polyangiitis) with glomerulonephritis and pulmonary involvement
next_steps: Perform full immunological workup including ANA, anti-dsDNA, anti-Smith, complement levels (C3, C4), and antiphospholipid antibodies (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta2-glycoprotein I)
Perform ultrasound-guided kidney biopsy for histological classification of glomerulonephritis
Initiate therapeutic anticoagulation for bilateral renal vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotics pending blood/sputum culture results to address potential superimposed infection
Evaluate for underlying malignancy as a potential trigger for nephrotic syndrome (paraneoplastic glomerulopathy)
Consult nephrology and rheumatology services for co-management

differential_diagnoses: Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) with lupus nephritis (Class V) and secondary nephrotic syndrome
Antiphospholipid syndrome (APS) presenting with catastrophic APS-like features (renal vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism)
Genetic/Familial nephropathy (e.g., FSGS) manifesting as nephrotic syndrome with secondary hypercoagulability
Infectious endocarditis with septic emboli and secondary glomerulonephritis
Primary membranous nephropathy with hypercoagulable state
next_steps: Initiate therapeutic anticoagulation immediately given bilateral renal vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism
Perform comprehensive autoimmune workup: ANA, anti-dsDNA, anti-Smith, C3, C4, and antiphospholipid antibody panel
Perform transthoracic/transesophageal echocardiogram to rule out intracardiac thrombus or infective endocarditis
Consult Nephrology to coordinate safe timing of renal biopsy considering the anticoagulation status
Collect serial blood and sputum cultures to guide antibiotic de-escalation
Obtain detailed family history regarding the sister's kidney disease and pursue genetic testing/counseling
